meta_title: Roofing in Sheridan, FL | Premier Impact Windows
meta_description: Expert roofing in Sheridan, FL. Hurricane-rated CertainTeed & West Lake systems, $0 down financing from $87/mo. Licensed, A+ BBB. Call Premier Impact Windows & Roofing today!
h1: Trusted Roofing Services in Sheridan, FL — Built to Withstand South Florida's Worst
intro:
Sheridan, Florida sits in the heart of Broward County, a tightly knit residential community where well-kept homes and tree-lined streets reflect the pride homeowners take in their properties. With average home values hovering around $350,000 and approximately half of all homes built before 1994, the roofing landscape in Sheridan tells a complex story — one of aging materials, changing building standards, and an ever-present need for hurricane-resilient protection. If your home predates the sweeping reforms that followed Hurricane Andrew, there's a strong chance your current roof was not engineered to meet today's Florida Building Code requirements for 170 mph wind zones. That's not a minor gap — it's the difference between walking away from a major storm with minimal damage and facing a catastrophic, insurance-complicated loss.

Located just minutes from Sheridan Park and bordered by Hollywood to the east, Pembroke Pines to the west, and Davie to the north, Sheridan's neighborhoods represent exactly the type of established, owner-occupied community that benefits most from investing in a proper roof system. We're not talking about a patch job or a quick re-shingle over existing layers — we're talking about a full, code-compliant roof replacement that adds real value to your home, reduces your insurance premiums, and gives you genuine peace of mind through every Atlantic hurricane season. With 9 named storms affecting Broward County since 1990, that kind of protection isn't a luxury. It's a necessity.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've completed more than 500 homes across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, and we understand what Sheridan rooftops face every single year. From punishing summer heat cycles that accelerate shingle granule loss to the sudden, violent wind events that can rip underlayment free in a matter of seconds, your roof is your home's first and most critical line of defense. Our team of in-house installation specialists — not subcontractors — brings over 60 years of combined experience to every project, ensuring that every nail, every course of shingles, and every ridge cap is installed precisely to manufacturer spec and Broward County Building Division standards. When you choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you're choosing the Kings of Service.

section_why.h2: Why Sheridan Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Roof Replacement
section_why.body:
There's no shortage of roofing contractors operating in Broward County, but not all of them offer what Premier Impact Windows & Roofing delivers as a standard — 100% in-house installation with a team that has seen, repaired, and replaced roofs across every neighborhood from Hollywood to Pembroke Pines. When you hire Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for your Sheridan roof replacement, you are not handing your project off to a rotating crew of day-labor subcontractors. You're working with a dedicated, licensed installation team that shows up on time, communicates clearly, and holds itself accountable from the first inspection to the final permit sign-off.

Our process begins with a thorough roof assessment, where we examine not just the surface shingles or tiles, but the decking beneath, the underlayment system, the flashing details around penetrations and valleys, and the overall ventilation setup of your attic. In South Florida's humid climate, improper ventilation is one of the leading contributors to premature roof failure, and it's a detail many budget contractors overlook entirely. We don't cut corners because we understand that your home in Sheridan is a significant investment — one worth protecting with the right materials and the right craftsmanship.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing carries an A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau, and we're fully licensed and insured throughout Florida. We work exclusively with industry-leading manufacturers — CertainTeed for architectural shingle systems and West Lake for concrete and clay tile applications — brands whose products are tested and rated for the exact wind conditions Sheridan experiences. Every roof we install is pulled with a permit through the Broward County Building Division, inspected, and documented, so your home remains fully insurable and compliant for years to come.

We also understand that financing a roof replacement can feel daunting for middle-income homeowners. That's why we offer flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down, making it genuinely possible to protect your home without draining your savings. From the first phone call to the final inspection, we earn the title 'Kings of Service' by treating every Sheridan homeowner with the attention and respect they deserve.

section_history.h2: Sheridan's Storm History Makes a Strong Roof Non-Negotiable
section_history.body:
Broward County — and Sheridan specifically — has been tested repeatedly by Atlantic hurricanes over the past three decades, and the record is sobering. Hurricane Andrew struck in August 1992, forever changing how Florida thinks about roofing standards. While Andrew's worst damage was concentrated in Miami-Dade, its winds reached well into Broward County, exposing the catastrophic vulnerability of pre-code construction. Thousands of roofs that had been considered adequate were torn from their structures entirely, leading directly to the sweeping Florida Building Code overhaul that followed. If your Sheridan home was built before 1994 — as roughly 50% of local homes were — and has never had a full roof replacement, it may still be operating under those outdated standards.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 delivered a different kind of blow. Tracking west-to-east across South Florida at unusual speed, Wilma generated sustained winds that stripped shingles across Broward County neighborhoods and left thousands of homes exposed to subsequent rainfall damage. Sheridan homeowners who had already upgraded to modern roofing systems fared significantly better — a direct, real-world validation of the investment. Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017, which served as another reminder that even 'near misses' carry enough wind energy to compromise aging roofs. Beyond those named storms, Broward County has experienced 9 significant storm events since 1990, each one placing cumulative stress on roof systems that were never designed for a multi-decade lifespan in a high-wind environment.

The takeaway for Sheridan homeowners is straightforward: South Florida does not offer roof complacency. Every hurricane season that passes without incident is an opportunity to get ahead of the risk — to replace an aging, sub-standard roof before the next storm makes that decision for you in the worst possible way.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Roofing in Sheridan and Broward County
section_code.body:
Sheridan falls within Broward County's jurisdiction for all building permits and inspections, meaning every roofing project — whether it's a full replacement or a significant repair — must be permitted through the Broward County Building Division and must comply with the Florida Building Code, 8th Edition (or current adopted version). While Sheridan is not located within the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), which covers Miami-Dade and Broward coastal areas, it is still subject to rigorous wind resistance requirements. The area is rated for 170 mph design wind speeds, which means all roofing products and installation methods must be tested and approved to perform under those load conditions.

For shingle roofing systems in Sheridan, the Florida Building Code requires a minimum of a six-nail fastening pattern for each shingle, along with a secondary water barrier — typically a self-adhering modified bitumen underlayment — that provides a last line of defense if wind-driven rain infiltrates beneath the shingles. This secondary water barrier requirement was introduced following the catastrophic roof losses in Hurricane Andrew and is non-negotiable for all permitted work in Broward County. Tile roofing systems face additional structural requirements, including engineered calculations to ensure the roof deck can support the weight of concrete or clay tile, proper mechanical fastening of each tile, and compliant foam adhesive applications where applicable.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on your behalf, from submitting the application to scheduling the Broward County Building Division inspections and ensuring final approval is documented. We never encourage homeowners to skip permits or perform unpermitted work — doing so puts your homeowner's insurance at risk, can complicate a future home sale, and leaves you with no legal protection if installation defects emerge. When we complete your Sheridan roof, it is done right, done legally, and done to last.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed Architectural Shingles and West Lake Tile — Premium Systems for Sheridan Homes
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two premium roofing systems ideally suited to the conditions Sheridan homeowners face: CertainTeed's Landmark Series architectural shingles and West Lake concrete and clay tile systems. Both product lines are engineered specifically for high-wind environments and carry the performance ratings required under Florida Building Code for 170 mph wind zones — giving Sheridan homeowners confidence that their new roof isn't just aesthetically appealing, but genuinely engineered to perform when it matters most.

The CertainTeed Landmark Series is one of the most respected architectural shingle lines in the roofing industry, and for good reason. These dimensional shingles feature a dual-layered construction that provides both visual depth and enhanced wind resistance. Landmark shingles are rated for winds up to 130 mph out of the box, with enhanced warranty options available for wind resistance at even higher speeds when installed with the manufacturer's required fastening patterns. The granule composition is designed to resist the intense UV radiation that accelerates shingle aging in South Florida's subtropical climate, and the SureStart PLUS warranty coverage provides robust protection against manufacturing defects. For Sheridan homeowners looking for a cost-effective, low-maintenance, and code-compliant roofing solution, CertainTeed Landmark is the benchmark.

For homeowners who prefer the classic aesthetic and exceptional durability of tile roofing — very common throughout Broward County's established neighborhoods — West Lake concrete and clay tile systems deliver outstanding performance. Concrete tile offers excellent impact and wind resistance with a heavier profile that helps anchor the roof system during high-wind events, while clay tile provides a lighter-weight, historically authentic option with superior longevity. Both West Lake product lines are Florida Product Approved and tested for the wind uplift conditions present in Sheridan. Tile roofs, when properly installed, routinely outlast the structures they protect, often carrying 40- to 50-year lifespans that far exceed shingle alternatives.

section_cost.h2: What Does a Roof Replacement Cost in Sheridan, FL — And How Can You Finance It?
section_cost.body:
For a Sheridan home with an average value of $350,000, a full roof replacement represents one of the most impactful investments you can make — both in terms of structural protection and financial return. Typical costs for a complete architectural shingle roof replacement on a standard Broward County single-family home range from approximately $12,000 to $20,000 depending on roof size, pitch complexity, and underlayment requirements. Concrete or clay tile systems generally range higher, from $18,000 to $35,000 or more, reflecting the additional material cost, structural engineering requirements, and installation labor involved. These are not frivolous expenses — they are investments in a critical structural system that directly affects your home's insurability, resale value, and safety.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes it easier to move forward with financing options designed for middle-income homeowners. With $0 down and monthly payment plans starting at just $87 per month, a new hurricane-rated roof doesn't have to mean depleting your emergency fund or delaying the project season after season while your existing roof continues to deteriorate. Our financing partners offer competitive rates with flexible terms, and our team will walk you through every available option during your free consultation.

From a return-on-investment perspective, a new roof in South Florida is among the highest-ROI home improvements you can make. Real estate studies consistently show that a new roof adds substantial value at the point of sale, often recovering 60% to 70% or more of its cost in increased home value — in a competitive Broward County market, a new code-compliant roof can be the deciding factor for buyers. Add in the insurance premium savings detailed in the next section, and the true cost of a Premier Impact Windows & Roofing roof replacement becomes considerably more favorable than the sticker price suggests.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings and Wind Mitigation Credits for Sheridan Homeowners
section_insurance.body:
One of the most financially compelling reasons to invest in a new hurricane-rated roof in Sheridan is the direct and significant impact it can have on your homeowner's insurance premiums. Florida's insurance market has been notoriously volatile in recent years, with carriers tightening underwriting standards and raising rates across Broward County. A home with an aging, non-compliant roof is increasingly difficult — and expensive — to insure. By contrast, a newly installed, permitted, hurricane-rated roof system can dramatically improve your insurability and qualify you for meaningful premium discounts.

The wind mitigation inspection process is central to unlocking these savings. After Premier Impact Windows & Roofing completes your roof replacement, a licensed wind mitigation inspector can evaluate your home and document the features that reduce hurricane risk — including the age of the roof, the type of roof covering, the secondary water barrier, and the roof deck attachment method. These findings are submitted to your insurance carrier on a standard OIR-B1-1802 form, and in many cases, Broward County homeowners see premium reductions of 20% to 40% or more following a qualifying roof replacement. For a policy costing $4,000 to $6,000 annually — not unusual in today's South Florida market — those savings can easily amount to $1,000 or more per year.

Major carriers operating in Broward County, including Citizens Property Insurance, Heritage Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, and others, all recognize the risk reduction associated with modern hurricane-rated roofing systems. Some carriers have made a new roof a condition of continued coverage for older homes, meaning the decision to replace an aging roof may not be entirely optional. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's team is experienced in working with homeowners to ensure all documentation — permits, product approvals, inspection reports — is organized and ready for submission to your insurance carrier, helping you capture every credit you've earned.

faqs.q: How do I know if my Sheridan home's roof needs to be fully replaced rather than repaired?
faqs.a: If your roof is more than 20 years old, has multiple areas of missing or cracked shingles, shows signs of granule loss in gutters, has active leaks, or was installed before Florida's post-Andrew building code reforms, a full replacement is almost always the more cost-effective long-term choice. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers free inspections to assess your specific situation and provide an honest recommendation — we'll never recommend a replacement you don't need.

faqs.q: What does a roof replacement in Sheridan typically cost, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: A full architectural shingle roof replacement in Sheridan typically ranges from $12,000 to $20,000, while tile systems range from $18,000 to $35,000 or more. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers $0 down financing with payments starting at $87 per month, making it accessible for middle-income homeowners. During your free consultation, we'll provide a detailed, itemized quote and walk you through all financing options so you can make the best decision for your budget.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my roof in Sheridan, and how does that process work?
faqs.a: Yes — all roof replacements in Sheridan require a permit through the Broward County Building Division. The permit ensures your new roof meets Florida Building Code requirements for 170 mph wind zones, including secondary water barriers and proper fastening patterns. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on your behalf, from application submission to scheduling the final inspection. All work is fully documented, ensuring your home remains insurable and compliant.

faqs.q: How long does a roof replacement take for a typical Sheridan home?
faqs.a: Most single-family roof replacements in Sheridan are completed in one to three days for shingle systems, depending on the size and complexity of the roof. Tile systems may take slightly longer due to the additional structural requirements and installation steps involved. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's in-house crews work efficiently and cleanly, and we coordinate inspections with the Broward County Building Division to minimize any delays between installation and final permit close-out.

faqs.q: Will a new roof lower my homeowner's insurance premium in Sheridan?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes — significantly. A new hurricane-rated roof with proper secondary water barriers qualifies for Florida's wind mitigation credits, which can reduce premiums by 20% to 40% or more with many Broward County carriers. After installation, a wind mitigation inspection documents your roof's protective features on a state-approved form submitted to your insurer. On a policy of $4,000 to $6,000 annually, that can mean $1,000 or more in annual savings — a powerful long-term return on your roofing investment.

cta_headline: Ready to Protect Your Sheridan Home with a Premier Roof?
cta_body: Contact Premier Impact Windows & Roofing today for your free, no-obligation roof inspection and quote — the Kings of Service are ready to put 60+ years of expertise to work for your Sheridan home, with $0 down financing and a team that handles everything from permit to final inspection.
